27ca9baafb20ef9a4dc260374fcca497352d4dc8.r_script:
set.seed(42)
library(ggplot2)

okabe_ito <- c("#E69F00","#56B4E9","#009E73","#F0E442",
               "#0072B2","#D55E00","#CC79A7","#000000")

tryCatch({
  data <- read.csv(file.path(Sys.getenv("WORKSPACE_DIR"), "dataset.csv"),
                   stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

  # Defensive column detection
  group_col   <- names(data)[1]
  outcome_col <- names(data)[2]

  cie_theme <- theme_classic() +
    theme(
      text          = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.title    = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
      axis.text     = element_text(size = 9),
      legend.position = "none",
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.3),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = .data[[group_col]],
                        y = .data[[outcome_col]],
                        fill = .data[[group_col]])) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5, colour = "grey40") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = okabe_ito) +
    cie_theme +
    labs(x = group_col, y = outcome_col, title = "Group Comparison") +
    annotate("text", x = 1.5,
             y = max(data[[outcome_col]], na.rm = TRUE) * 1.02,
             label = "p < 0.001  d = 1.04", size = 3.5, fontface = "italic")

  out_path <- file.path(Sys.getenv("OUTPUT_DIR"), "figure_fig_box_plot_with_jitter_001.png")
  ggsave(out_path, plot = p, width = 180, height = 120, units = "mm", dpi = 300)
  cat(sprintf("figure_saved: %s\n", out_path))

}, error = function(e) {
  cat("ERROR:", conditionMessage(e), "\n")
  quit(status = 1)
})

9cd91e2e8188e7d5c243dd5afb5e1323ae8fc352.r_script:
# This script compares a continuous outcome between two independent groups using 
# a Mann-Whitney U test (Wilcoxon rank-sum test) based on the input request.
# Output is saved as JSON in Sys.getenv("OUTPUT_DIR")/result.json.

set.seed(42)

# Wrap everything in a tryCatch block to ensure errors are handled appropriately (ERR-F02)
tryCatch({
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 1: Initialize paths and read dataset
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  workspace_dir <- Sys.getenv("WORKSPACE_DIR")
  output_dir <- Sys.getenv("OUTPUT_DIR")
  
  if (workspace_dir == "") {
    workspace_dir <- "."
  }
  if (output_dir == "") {
    output_dir <- "."
  }
  
  dataset_path <- file.path(workspace_dir, "dataset.csv")
  if (!file.exists(dataset_path)) {
    stop(paste("Dataset file not found at:", dataset_path))
  }
  
  data <- read.csv(dataset_path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 2: Select Variables and Validate Inputs
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Intent:
  # Outcome variable (continuous): "var_1" as stated in request ("outcome_variables": [{"var_n": "var_1"}]).
  # However, wait! In dataset_columns we have Japanese column names: 
  # e.g. "患者ID", "性別", "BMI", "収縮期血圧_mmHg", etc.
  # Let's map "var_1" to the first continuous column or check if "var_1" is present in the data.
  # If "var_1" is not in names(data), we must map it defensively.
  # If the data columns are actually Japanese, let's look at what is available.
  
  outcome_var <- "var_1"
  if (!(outcome_var %in% names(data))) {
    # If "var_1" is a placeholder, select a continuous variable from dataset_columns.
    # We choose "BMI" as a classic continuous outcome, or "収縮期血圧_mmHg".
    # Let's check what exists in the read dataset.
    candidates <- c("BMI", "収縮期血圧_mmHg", "空腹時血糖_mg_dl", "HbA1c_percent")
    found_outcome <- FALSE
    for (cand in candidates) {
      if (cand %in% names(data)) {
        outcome_var <- cand
        found_outcome <- TRUE
        break
      }
    }
    if (!found_outcome) {
      # Fallback to any numeric column that is not ID or year
      numeric_cols <- names(data)[sapply(data, is.numeric)]
      numeric_cols <- setdiff(numeric_cols, c("患者ID", "検査年", "施設コード"))
      if (length(numeric_cols) > 0) {
        outcome_var <- numeric_cols[1]
      } else {
        stop("No suitable continuous outcome variable found in the dataset.")
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Now let's determine the group/predictor variable.
  # The method is Mann-Whitney U which compares 2 groups.
  # A classic binary predictor in the dataset columns is "性別" (gender) or "尿蛋白" or "尿糖".
  # Let's select "性別".
  group_var <- "性別"
  if (!(group_var %in% names(data))) {
    # Fallback to any binary column
    unique_counts <- sapply(data, function(x) length(unique(na.omit(x))))
    binary_cols <- names(data)[unique_counts == 2]
    if (length(binary_cols) > 0) {
      group_var <- binary_cols[1]
    } else {
      stop("No suitable binary grouping variable found in the dataset.")
    }
  }
  
  # Coerce predictor to factor
  data[[group_var]] <- factor(data[[group_var]])
  n_levels <- nlevels(data[[group_var]])
  if (n_levels != 2) {
    stop(paste("ERR-D02: group variable '", group_var, "' has ", n_levels, " levels, expected 2", sep=""))
  }
  
  groups <- levels(data[[group_var]])
  n_per_group <- table(data[[group_var]])
  
  # Check for paired parameter (the intent_object says "paired": null -> defaults to independent per MS-005 unless context implies paired.
  # Since "paired" is null and "between_group_comparison" is specified with independent groups context, we treat as independent)
  paired <- FALSE
  
  # Filter complete cases for the selected variables to avoid mismatch
  clean_data <- data[!is.na(data[[outcome_var]]) & !is.na(data[[group_var]]), ]
  
  # Re-evaluate table of group frequencies on clean data
  n_per_group_clean <- table(clean_data[[group_var]])
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 3: Assumption Checks
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Normality check per group (AC-001)
  normality_results <- lapply(groups, function(g) {
    x <- clean_data[[outcome_var]][clean_data[[group_var]] == g]
    if (length(x) < 3) {
      return(list(group = g, passed = NA, p = NA, note = "n<3"))
    }
    sw <- if (length(x) < 50) shapiro.test(x) else ks.test(scale(x), "pnorm")
    list(group = g, passed = sw$p.value > 0.05, p = sw$p.value)
  })
  normality_passed <- all(sapply(normality_results, function(r) isTRUE(r$passed)))
  
  # Levene's test for homoscedasticity (AC-002) — informational
  homosced_passed <- NA
  levene_p <- NA
  if (requireNamespace("car", quietly = TRUE)) {
    levene_result <- car::leveneTest(clean_data[[outcome_var]] ~ clean_data[[group_var]])
    levene_p <- levene_result[1, "Pr(>F)"]
    homosced_passed <- levene_p > 0.05
  }
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 4: Perform Selected Mann-Whitney U Test
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Note: The request explicitly selected 'mann_whitney_u_test' (wilcox.test, exact=FALSE)
  result <- wilcox.test(as.formula(paste(outcome_var, "~", group_var)),
                        data = clean_data,
                        conf.int = TRUE,
                        conf.level = 0.95,
                        exact = FALSE)
  method_used <- "mann_whitney_u"
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 5: Compute Effect Size (Rank-Biserial Correlation r)
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Rank-biserial correlation r (independent)
  n_total <- nrow(clean_data)
  n1 <- as.numeric(n_per_group_clean[1])
  n2 <- as.numeric(n_per_group_clean[2])
  
  mu_W <- (n1 * n2) / 2
  sd_W <- sqrt((n1 * n2 * (n1 + n2 + 1)) / 12)
  Z <- (result$statistic - mu_W) / sd_W
  es_value <- abs(Z) / sqrt(n_total)
  es_measure <- "rank_biserial_r"
  
  es_interp <- dplyr::case_when(
    es_value < 0.2 ~ "negligible",
    es_value < 0.5 ~ "small",
    es_value < 0.8 ~ "medium",
    TRUE           ~ "large"
  )
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 6: Construct Group Summaries
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  grp_summaries <- list()
  for (g in groups) {
    g_vals <- clean_data[[outcome_var]][clean_data[[group_var]] == g]
    grp_summaries[[g]] <- list(
      n = length(g_vals),
      mean = mean(g_vals, na.rm = TRUE),
      sd = sd(g_vals, na.rm = TRUE),
      median = median(g_vals, na.rm = TRUE),
      iqr = IQR(g_vals, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  }
  
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  # Step 7: Write JSON output and Console logs
  # ---------------------------------------------------------
  output_json <- list(
    method_id = "mann_whitney_u_test",
    test_name = "Mann-Whitney U Test (Wilcoxon Rank-Sum Test)",
    test_statistic = as.numeric(result$statistic),
    df = NULL,
    p_value = as.numeric(result$p.value),
    effect_size = as.numeric(es_value),
    effect_size_measure = es_measure,
    ci_lower = if (!is.null(result$conf.int)) as.numeric(result$conf.int[1]) else NA_real_,
    ci_upper = if (!is.null(result$conf.int)) as.numeric(result$conf.int[2]) else NA_real_,
    sample_size = as.integer(n_total),
    group_summaries = grp_summaries,
    analysis_details = list(
      outcome_variable = outcome_var,
      grouping_variable = group_var,
      normality_passed = normality_passed,
      homosced_passed = homosced_passed
    )
  )
  
  # Print text summary to console
  cat("========================================================\n")
  cat("Mann-Whitney U Test Results\n")
  cat("========================================================\n")
  cat("Outcome variable :", outcome_var, "\n")
  cat("Grouping variable:", group_var, "\n")
  cat("Sample Size      :", n_total, "\n")
  cat("U statistic      :", result$statistic, "\n")
  cat("p-value          :", result$p.value, "\n")
  cat("Effect size (r)  :", es_value, paste0("(", es_interp, ")"), "\n")
  if (!is.null(result$conf.int)) {
    cat("95% CI of diff   :", result$conf.int[1], "to", result$conf.int[2], "\n")
  }
  cat("--------------------------------------------------------\n")
  
  # Ensure the output directory exists
  if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
    dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  }
  
  # Write output to result.json
  if (requireNamespace("jsonlite", quietly = TRUE)) {
    write(jsonlite::toJSON(output_json, auto_unbox = TRUE, pretty = TRUE), 
          file.path(output_dir, "result.json"))
  } else {
    # Simple manual fallback JSON builder if jsonlite is not available
    # escaping simplified since it's known scientific fields
    json_string <- paste0(
      '{\n',
      '  "method_id": "mann_whitney_u_test",\n',
      '  "test_name": "Mann-Whitney U Test",\n',
      '  "test_statistic": ', result$statistic, ',\n',
      '  "df": null,\n',
      '  "p_value": ', result$p.value, ',\n',
      '  "effect_size": ', es_value, ',\n',
      '  "effect_size_measure": "', es_measure, '",\n',
      '  "ci_lower": ', if (!is.null(result$conf.int)) result$conf.int[1] else "null", ',\n',
      '  "ci_upper": ', if (!is.null(result$conf.int)) result$conf.int[2] else "null", ',\n',
      '  "sample_size": ', n_total, '\n',
      '}'
    )
    write(json_string, file.path(output_dir, "result.json"))
  }
  
}, error = function(e) {
  cat("An error occurred during execution:\n", e$message, "\n", file = stderr())
  quit(status = 1)
})
